summary: Jeff Atwood vents frustration about Windows XP's automatic update feature, which nags users every ten minutes to restart after installing updates. The dialog box cannot be dismissed and offers only 'Restart Now' or 'Restart Later' options, with the latter simply delaying the inevitable by ten minutes. Even killing the responsible process (wuauclt.exe) doesn't work as it respawns. Atwood crowdsources solutions from commenters and presents two fixes: stopping the Automatic Updates service or modifying Group Policy settings to disable the auto-restart nagging.
key_points: Windows XP's update restart dialog is undismissable and reappears every ten minutes if postponed
The wuauclt.exe process responsible for the nagging respawns when killed, like a 'bad zombie movie'
Users can stop the 'Automatic Updates' service temporarily via command line or Services panel
Group Policy settings offer a permanent fix by disabling auto-restart or extending the prompt interval
money_quotes: This is perhaps the Naggiest. Dialog. Box. Ever.
If you click Restart Later, it pops up again ten minutes later, like clockwork.
I tried killing wuauclt.exe, and like a bad zombie movie, it keeps coming back.
I want automatic updates, but I also want to restart my computer when I feel like it.
key_insight: Good security features become user-hostile when they prioritize the system's needs over the user's workflow and autonomy.
